host_actions: convergence is HIGH — reframe task as failure-conditions question (HOST_QUESTIONING_DEPTH Rule 2) and re-run deliberate

self_judge_bias: judge "anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5" shares provider "anthropic" with at least one worker — convergence and ranking verdicts may be biased toward same-provider models. Re-run with a cross-provider judge for a sanity check.

convergence.reasoning: All three analysts identify the same core themes—organizational/team maturity, domain boundary clarity, and operational/infrastructure capability—as conditions where MSA fails relative to monolith. While they prioritize these themes differently (Haiku emphasizes small teams + coupling issues, Grok separates team size from DevOps maturity, Gemini leads with unclear domains), they converge on the fundamental diagnosis: MSA requires organizational readiness that small/immature teams typically lack, and fails when domain architecture isn't clear or when business constraints (ACID consistency, low latency) conflict with distributed design assumptions.
